hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_title: Past & Present — Talking About What Changed
lesson.menu_desc: Reviewing the year on the desk: what happened last year vs what has changed since -- past simple against present perfect -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- Past & Present: Talking About What Changed
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 7 -- Past & Present

listenings.text: Hello, Sergio. This is Marina, with a short review of how the desk's risk profile has changed this year, before the committee meeting. Last year was hard. In the first quarter, exposure climbed sharply to a new peak after a market shock, and volatility rose fast. Then the desk cut the biggest positions in the spring. Since March, the picture has improved month by month. The number of limit breaches has dropped to zero this year, and we have kept a clean track record with the regulator. The trend has been steady for six months now. One thing has not changed yet: the credit desk has not sent the final figures, so please chase them before Friday. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note before your review. I looked at the numbers again this morning. Last year our provisions rose a lot, but they have come down every quarter since January, which is good news for the committee. The stress-test results have improved as well, and the buffer has been stable for months. I did notice one dip in April, but the trend recovered fast after that. So far, no new deficiency has appeared this year. I have already updated the slides, so you have the latest figures. Can you call me after the meeting? Thanks.

extra_audio.text: Last year, exposure climbed sharply to a new peak. In the first quarter, the desk cut the biggest positions. Since March, the risk profile has improved month by month. The number of limit breaches has dropped to zero this year. So far, the committee has approved every plan on time.
